subject: Cloud Computing
title: The Three-Tier Architecture
description: Learn The Three-Tier Architecture in one minute.

hook.text: Presentation, logic, data — three layers that have powered web applications for over 25 years.

buildup.text: The frontend shows the UI. The backend processes logic. The database stores data. Each tier scales independently.

discovery.text: Cloud makes three-tier easy: a CDN for the frontend, containers for the backend, managed database for data.

twist.text: Modern apps are outgrowing three tiers. Caching layers, message queues, and APIs add more complexity between them.

climax.text: Three-tier is a starting point, not a ceiling. Most production systems evolve into something richer over time.

punchline.text: Three tiers got us this far. Real systems need more layers.

quiz.question: What are the three tiers in traditional three-tier architecture?
